name: Payment History
description: Payment records table with date range filtering, export, and receipt generation. Supports tenant, property, and portfolio views.

portfolio.description: All payments across the full property portfolio with date range filters and export controls

tenant.description: Single tenant payment history filtered to one tenant with simplified columns and receipt links

property.description: Property-wide payment history aggregating all tenant payments for a single property

visual_brief: Data table of payment records with columns: date, description, property/unit, payer, amount (green for received, red for refunded), and status badge (completed, pending, failed). A header row contains the title, date range filter (date pickers or preset buttons like 30d/90d/1y), and an export button. A summary row at the top or bottom shows total received, total outstanding, and period comparison. The portfolio preset adds a property column. The tenant preset filters to a single tenant. The property preset filters to a single property.

responsive.mobile: Table becomes a card list — each payment as a stacked card showing date, description, amount, and status. Filters collapse to a filter button. Export moves to a menu.
responsive.tablet: Table with horizontal scroll for extra columns. Date range filter visible in the header.
responsive.desktop: Full table with all columns visible. Summary row and filters always visible.

layout_hints.financial_priority: Keep collected totals, date range, and current payment status easy to scan first. Secondary metadata like method or payer should support that read, not compete with it.
layout_hints.table_discipline: The pattern owns the payments table and filter rhythm. Parent finance pages should place broader KPI framing above it rather than nesting this inside another table-like shell.
layout_hints.mobile_translation: When collapsing to cards, keep date, amount, and status grouped in a way that still feels like a ledger rather than a generic feed.

motion.micro: Summary values or status badges may brighten subtly on hover and filter interactions may clarify the active state without distracting from the ledger.
motion.transitions: Date-range or filter changes should cross-fade rows or update totals smoothly so the payment history still feels stable and trustworthy.

keyboard: Tab through filters, export actions, and then the payment rows or cards in reading order.
announcements: Amount, date, status, and payer/property context should be exposed semantically so payment records remain understandable without table styling alone.

composition.PaymentHistory: Container(d-section, flex-col, gap-4) > [Header + Filters? + SummaryRow? + PaymentTable]
composition.SummaryRow: Row(d-data) > [TotalReceived(mono-data, color: success) + TotalOutstanding(mono-data, color: warning)]
composition.PaymentRow: Row(d-data-row) > [Date + Description + Property? + Payer + Amount(mono-data, color: status) + StatusBadge(d-annotation)]
